description: False-colour satellite imagery depicting a coastal landscape. The deep blue area on the right represents the sea, bordered by a narrow, light beige strip of land, likely a beach or sand dunes. Inland, large expanses of vibrant red signify healthy, dense vegetation, typical of false-colour infrared composites indicating high chlorophyll content, such as forests or vigorous crops. Within these red vegetated areas, elongated, parallel streaks of lighter greyish-beige suggest different vegetation types, bare ground, or less dense cover. A prominent dark, winding river or channel system flows through the vegetated land and connects to the coast. In the bottom-left corner, distinct geometric parcels in shades of red and lighter beige denote agricultural fields. No scale, specific location, or date information is visible.
